programs.name: MIT MITES (MOSTEC)
programs.description: Immerse yourself in a 6-week residential engineering experience at MIT designed specifically for high-achieving minority and first-generation students. Work in state-of-the-art labs, collaborate with peers from across the country, and receive mentoring from MIT students and faculty. Develop technical skills, build lasting professional networks, and gain confidence in your ability to succeed in rigorous STEM environments.
programs.advisorNote: MITES is one of the most prestigious pathways for underrepresented students in STEM—admission demonstrates exceptional potential and commitment. Colleges view this program as a strong signal of both academic excellence and diversity contribution.

programs.name: Stanford Summer Research College (SIMR)
programs.description: Spend 8 weeks conducting original research at Stanford across engineering, computer science, chemistry, and biology. Work directly with PhD graduate students and faculty mentors on real projects, participate in seminars, and present your findings. Gain deep lab experience, understand graduate-level research workflows, and build relationships with researchers in your field of interest.
programs.advisorNote: SIMR provides genuine research experience that directly strengthens STEM college applications—especially valuable if you choose a project you can discuss in detail during interviews. The research credential carries weight with top universities.

programs.name: Research Science Institute (RSI)
programs.description: Attend a highly selective 4-week summer program bringing together exceptional STEM students from around the world. Dive deep into advanced physics, computer science, or mathematics through intensive coursework and research projects. Experience dorm life with international peers, attend seminars from leading scientists, and work on problems that push the boundaries of these fields.
programs.advisorNote: RSI's global reputation and rigorous curriculum make it exceptionally valuable for STEM applications. Colleges recognize the program's selectivity—admission itself becomes a talking point in your application narrative.

programs.name: COSMOS (California State Summer School for Mathematics & Science)
programs.description: Spend 4 weeks at a UC campus (Davis, Irvine, or Santa Cruz) exploring specialized STEM topics through daily lectures, hands-on labs, and field trips. Choose a cluster matching your interests and work alongside peers with similar passions. Engage in project-based learning, interact with UC faculty, and experience university-level coursework while living on campus.
programs.advisorNote: COSMOS is an excellent option for California students seeking affordable, quality STEM enrichment. While less selective than MIT MITES or RSI, it still demonstrates serious interest in STEM and provides solid research foundations.

programs.name: PROMYS (Program in Mathematics for Young Scientists)
programs.description: Explore pure mathematics over 6 weeks through a residential program emphasizing mathematical proof and problem-solving. Investigate deep topics in number theory, abstract algebra, and combinatorics. Study alongside gifted young mathematicians, learn from university faculty, and develop the kind of rigorous mathematical thinking valued in higher mathematics.
programs.advisorNote: PROMYS is the go-to program for students serious about advanced mathematics—colleges view it as strong evidence of mathematical maturity. Ideal if you're considering math, physics, or computer science at selective universities.

programs.name: Pacific Science Center Camps for Curious Minds
programs.description: Over 100 unique STEAM camp experiences across six Seattle-area locations for PreK-8. Themes include robotics, dissection lab, environmental engineering, and more. Sensory-friendly and girls-focused options available. Scholarships cover registration, lunches, and afternoon care through the Access to Science Pipeline.

programs.name: Museum of Flight ACE (Aerospace Camp Experience)
programs.description: Week-long aerospace camps at the Museum of Flight in Seattle for K-9th graders. Hands-on activities in aviation and space including group projects, experiments, visits from aerospace professionals, and museum exhibit exploration. Runs June 22 through August 28, 2026.

programs.name: DigiPen Open World Summer Workshops (Ages 5-12)
programs.description: Hands-on STEAM workshops at DigiPen's Redmond campus for ages 5-12 covering game design, programming, robotics, art, and animation. Age-appropriate tracks tailored to 5-9 and 10-12 age groups. Girls-focused STEM tracks also available.

programs.name: Seattle MESA (Mathematics, Engineering, Science Achievement)
programs.description: Year-round STEM enrichment for grades 6-12 with after-school tutoring, statewide STEM competitions, mentorship, and a 3-week summer math scholars program for incoming freshmen. Emphasis on traditionally underrepresented students in STEM fields. MESA periods during the school day let advisors work with students on academics.

programs.name: UW Engineering Discovery Days
programs.provider: University of Washington College of Engineering
programs.description: Annual 2-day spring event oriented toward 4th-8th grade students that draws up to 10,000 visitors to UW campus. UW engineering students and faculty share their work through hands-on activities, introducing students to engineering disciplines and research.

programs.name: UW Engineering Academy
programs.provider: University of Washington College of Engineering
programs.description: Free one-week summer program at UW Seattle preparing Washington state students for engineering careers. July 20-24, 2026, 10AM-4PM. Must be a rising 10th or 11th grader attending high school in the greater Seattle area.

programs.name: UW Changemakers in Computing (CiC)
programs.provider: University of Washington Paul G. Allen School
programs.description: Free summer program for rising juniors and seniors at Washington state high schools. Develop coding skills through culturally relevant, project-based learning, explore areas of computing, and discover changemaking impact through technology.

programs.name: Museum of Flight Aeronautical Explorations
programs.description: Free formal credit-bearing CTE summer school course for rising 9th-12th graders. June 29 - July 17, 2026, 8:30AM-3:30PM M-F. Earn 0.5 high school CTE credits. Includes graded tests, quizzes, and projects equivalent to a semester course. Must be a WA state resident.

programs.name: DigiPen WANIC Summer Programs
programs.description: Free state-funded 3-week program at DigiPen's Redmond campus. June 29 - July 17, 2026, 8AM-3PM M-F. Covers game design, programming, digital art, and animation. No tuition or fees.

programs.name: Robinson Center Saturday Enrichment Program
programs.provider: UW Robinson Center for Young Scholars
programs.description: Saturday enrichment courses at UW for academically advanced students. Offerings span STEM, humanities, and creative arts. Hosted at the UW Guggenheim Annex. Spring 2026 offerings available with registration opening in March.

programs.name: UW Youth & Teen Programs
programs.description: Remote and in-person enrichment courses for teens in coding, data visualization, writing, and more. Four sessions offered throughout the year. Summer 2026 courses available.

programs.name: Washington MESA College Prep Program
programs.provider: University of Washington Office of Minority Affairs
programs.description: Statewide program with centers across Washington engaging middle and high school students in STEM through after-school tutoring, competitions, mentorship. Emphasis on underrepresented students. Summer math scholars program for incoming freshmen.

programs.name: Destination Science Summer Camps — Seattle
programs.description: Week-long in-person STEM camps across the Seattle area with hands-on science projects. Multiple 2026 themes available. Focuses on experiential learning in science for elementary students.

programs.name: Rice University STEM-Letics Academy
programs.description: Week-long summer academy at Rice University for 3rd-5th graders combining STEM and physical activities to keep students mentally and physically active. Offered multiple weeks during summer.

programs.name: Camp Invention — Texas Locations
programs.description: Nationally recognized summer day camp with hands-on STEM adventures led by local educators. Held at schools and community centers across Texas. Activities designed by National Inventors Hall of Fame inductees.

programs.name: Rice Bio Academies
programs.description: Immersive hands-on summer experience at Rice University for 7th-11th graders offered in three focused sections: BioTech, BioMed, and BioEng. Students work in Rice research labs alongside faculty.

programs.name: Houston PREP (Prefreshman Enrichment Program)
programs.description: Free summer enrichment for grades 6-12 aimed at enhancing academic backgrounds of socially and economically disadvantaged students in STEM. Long-running Houston program.

programs.name: Girls Inc. Dallas Eureka! STEM Program
programs.description: Free STEM enrichment program for 7th-8th grade girls in the Dallas metro area. Multi-year program with summer intensive and year-round activities exploring science, technology, engineering, and math careers.

programs.name: Governor's Summer Merit Program — Texas
programs.description: State-sponsored summer camp motivating Texas high school students to consider STEM careers. Students explore multiple STEM fields and are introduced to in-demand skilled STEM jobs.

programs.name: LA's BEST Summer Learning Program
programs.description: Free full-day summer enrichment at select LAUSD elementary schools. Keeps children safe, healthy, and learning all summer with STEAM activities, field trips, and recreation. Runs several weeks in June and July.

programs.name: Club SciKidz Silicon Valley
programs.description: Hands-on STEM summer camps in Silicon Valley with experiments and interactive projects. Curriculum designed to inspire a love of science, technology, and engineering in young learners.

programs.name: COSMOS (CA State Summer School for Math & Science)
programs.description: Prestigious free residential summer program at six UC campuses (Davis, Irvine, LA, Merced, San Diego, Santa Cruz). Students explore STEM topics, work with renowned faculty, and conduct research in state-of-the-art facilities. Grades 8-12.

programs.name: SAGE (Science Accelerating Girls' Engagement in STEM)
programs.description: Free one-week residential camp for Northern California public school students. Students make professional connections, learn about scientific discoveries and engineering, and explore STEM careers. Open to freshmen through juniors.

programs.name: Camp CHLA (Children's Hospital Los Angeles)
programs.description: Free 5-day immersive healthcare exploration at Children's Hospital LA for LA County freshmen-juniors. Meet medical professionals, observe clinical settings, and gain firsthand healthcare experience.

programs.name: CYC Summer Programs (Community Youth Center SF)
programs.provider: Community Youth Center of San Francisco
programs.description: Free summer program with weekly field trips and hands-on, age-appropriate curriculum integrating literacy, math, STEAM, and recreation. Supports students academically, socially, and emotionally.

programs.name: Summer Rising 2026
programs.description: Free citywide summer learning and enrichment for K-8 students in NYC. Teacher-led ELA and math instruction, plus enrichment in STEM, arts, financial literacy, sports, and field trips. Elementary runs 7 weeks (Jul 1 - Aug 14), middle school runs 6 weeks (Jul 1 - Aug 7).

programs.name: STEM Matters NYC Enrichment Programs
programs.provider: NYC Department of Education / Cultural Institutions
programs.description: Free summer enrichment at cultural institutions across all five NYC boroughs. One-week camps and multi-week high school programs for grades 1-12 in NYC public and charter schools. Spring and summer break programming.

programs.name: Cooper Union Summer STEM 2026
programs.description: Free 6-week in-person STEM program at Cooper Union (July 6 - Aug 13, 2026, M-Th 9AM-3PM). Open to high school students worldwide with priority for NYC residents. Rigorous engineering and science curriculum at 41 Cooper Square.

programs.name: Columbia University S-PREP (State Pre-College Enrichment)
programs.description: Free high school and college preparatory program for students interested in medicine or related STEM fields. Includes both summer and academic year sessions at Columbia University. Strong pathway for pre-med students.

programs.name: Columbia Double Discovery Center Summer Academy
programs.description: Free 4-week academic enrichment commuter program at Columbia University for 9th-10th graders in NYC. Immersive college campus experience with courses in reading, writing, and research skills.

programs.name: New York Edge Summer Camp
programs.description: Free summer program offering STEM, arts, sports, and academic enrichment to NYC public school students. Community-based programming at schools across the city.

programs.name: Ann Arbor Hands-On Museum Summer Camps
programs.description: Hands-on science summer camps for K-8 at the Ann Arbor Hands-On Museum. Students explore robotics, forensics, flight, and more through experiential science education.

programs.name: Henry Ford Summer Camps
programs.description: Summer camps at The Henry Ford for grades 2-7 featuring artifact explorations and unique learning experiences. June 15 - Aug 28, 2026. Students explore innovation, invention, and American ingenuity through hands-on activities.

programs.name: Michigan Science Center Summer Camps
programs.description: STEM summer camps at the Michigan Science Center in Detroit exploring earth science, animals, engineering, LEGO building, and astronaut skills. Hands-on experiments and interactive learning.

programs.name: Michigan Tech Summer Youth Programs
programs.description: Week-long residential and day camps at Michigan Tech covering engineering, science, computing, and outdoor adventures. Middle and high school programs available. Students explore STEM disciplines on a university campus.

programs.name: EMU Engage K-12 Camp Programs
programs.description: Range of academic and STEM summer camps at Eastern Michigan University for K-12 students. Programs include science, technology, arts, and enrichment activities on the EMU campus.

programs.name: NYLF Explore STEM (Middle School)
programs.description: Week-long residential STEM program for middle schoolers at university campuses including Loyola, University of Miami, Villanova, UT Dallas, Rice, and UC San Diego. Hands-on activities: coding robots, solving crime scenes, dissecting lungs. Connect with industry professionals.

programs.name: NSLC (National Student Leadership Conference)
programs.description: 30 different pre-college career exploration programs across 13 university campuses. Programs run 6, 9, or 18 days covering medicine, engineering, law, business, and more. Since 1989, NSLC has been a leading provider of experiential learning for high schoolers.

programs.name: Camp Invention (National)
programs.description: Nationally recognized STEM day camp with hands-on invention activities at 1,500+ school and community locations. Curriculum designed by National Inventors Hall of Fame inductees. Led by local certified educators.

programs.name: NYLF Pathways to STEM (Elementary)
programs.description: Residential STEM experience for elementary students (grades 3-5) introducing pathways in science, technology, engineering, and math through hands-on experiments, team challenges, and college campus exploration.

programs.name: Washington STEM Network Programs
programs.description: Statewide nonprofit connecting students, schools, and communities to STEM opportunities across Washington. Regional STEM networks coordinate local programs, resources, and career-connected learning from elementary through high school.

programs.name: Seattle Public Schools Expanded Learning Summer
programs.description: Free full-day summer program providing targeted academic support and enrichment five days per week. Available to SPS students, with priority for students scoring below 60th percentile in Math and Reading.
